remaining_tasks.name: Resolve open blocker (carried from prior session): my-solution:v1 CheatCode policy requires zenoh transport; host humble fastrtps engine + kilted zenoh model can't discover each other
mitigation_paths: (a) Host-side zenohd + fastdds↔zenoh bridge — ~30min — risks 'no bridges' architectural law
(b) Host-build aic_model humble-native (mirror 04-03 pivot) — ~60-90min — cleanest path; preserves architectural law
(c) Stub lifecycle node mimicking /aic_model/get_state for PARITY-07 fire-test only — ~20min — discriminator, not full M1

decisions.decision: REQUIREMENTS.md inline checkboxes are PRIMARY truth source; traceability table is DERIVED and was stale
decisions.rationale: Phase 3 + Phase 4 plan landings updated inline checkboxes but skipped traceability table refresh; root cause of all bad-progress reports this session. Fix: every phase closure must atomically refresh table + STATE + ROADMAP

decisions.decision: Forward-pull + backward-pull (backlog sweep) discipline formalized with 3 enforcement checkpoints + atomic-closure rule
decisions.rationale: User-flagged pattern: deferrals got punted phase-to-phase but never picked up. Forward-pull was soft guideline never enforced. Phase boundaries are scope organizers, not capability fences

decisions.decision: Plan 04-3.5 inserted between 04-03 and 04-04 to bundle 5 CLOSE-NOW runtime items as a gate
decisions.rationale: Without these closures, Plan 04-04's parity_report.py would flag every trial FAIL on runtime-gate failures rather than real Isaac-Sim-vs-Gazebo divergence. Closing first gives parity_report a meaningful first-run signal

decisions.decision: G03 + G04 ROADMAP gap-plans marked [SUPERSEDED] (not deleted, not [x])
decisions.rationale: Both drafted before commit 079afd8 rclpy pivot; describe an OGN-based architecture the codebase no longer takes. Plan files preserved as historical record + diagnostic trail showing why pivot was necessary. Verified 2026-05-08 by reading both bodies + parity_publishers.py source

decisions.decision: GSD upstream contribution path: prototype locally first, then PR to gsd-build/get-shit-done as opt-in feature
decisions.rationale: User noted forward-pull is mandatory for robotics but software projects may not need it. Upstream design = config flag workflow.surface_adjacency_required (default false) so it's strictly additive and more likely to get approved-feature label

next_action: On resume: read this HANDOFF.json + .continue-here.md + STATE.md + CLAUDE.md autonomous-mode block (per session-bootstrap rule). Then EITHER (a) start Plan 04-3.5 Task 1 (PARITY-09 motion deficit hunt — recommended; sim-side; doesn't need user decision) OR (b) prompt user for model-zenoh mitigation path choice. context_notes: This session was triggered by user's runtime audit request after noticing the prior progress reports kept being wrong. Discovery cascade: duplicate spawn namespace → wrench garbage zeros → wrist cameras never published → arm joint drives wrong → cable doesn't fall → kinematic task-board → controller subscriber 100% broken (PARITY-09). Root cause of bad-progress-reports was stale traceability table never refreshed since Phase 2 closure. Major policy work: backlog-sweep + forward-pull enforcement + payload-sanity gate + status-reading discipline ALL added to CLAUDE.md. First-dogfood backlog sweep on Phase 4 produced 04-BACKLOG-SWEEP.md with 23 deferrals + 4-status decisions. Plan 04-3.5 created to close 5 CLOSE-NOW items before resuming 04-04. Process discoveries (clunky bits) captured for upstream GSD PR — opt-in via config flag. Mood: methodical, defensive, user is asking right questions; trust in artifacts is being rebuilt by reconciling truth state.
